body_excerpt: {"credits": [{"type": "finder", "user": {"avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/229667752?v=4", "events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/smiotani-aeyesec/events{/privacy}", "followers_url": "https://api.github.com/users/smiotani-aeyesec/followers", "following_url": "https://api.github.com/users/smiotani-aeyesec/following{/other_user}", "gists_url": "https://api.github.com/users/smiotani-aeyesec/gists{/gist_id}", "gravatar_id": "", "html_url": "https://github.com/smiotani-aeyesec", "id": 229667752, "login": "smiotani-aeyesec", "node_id": "U_kgDODbBzqA", "organizations_url": "https://api.github.com/users/smiotani-aeyesec/orgs", "received_events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/smiotani-aeyesec/received_events", "repos_url": "https://api.github.com/users/smiotani-aeyesec/repos", "site_admin": false, "starred_url": "https://api.github.com/users/smiotani-aeyesec/starred{/owner}{/repo}", "subscriptions_url": "https://api.github.com/users/smiotani-aeyesec/subscriptions", "type": "User", "url": "https://api.github.com/users/smiotani-aeyesec", "user_view_type": "public"}}], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-41249", "cvss": {"score": 8.2, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:L/A:N"}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 8.2, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:L/A:N"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-94", "name": "Improper Control of Generation of Code ('Code Injection')"}], "description": "### Summary The GitHub Actions workflow (`.github/workflows/static.yml`) uses the `pull_request_target` trigger but dangerously checks out the unverified code from the pull request head (`ref: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.ref }}`). Subsequently, it executes a script (`bin/console`) from this untrusted checkout. This allows any external attacker to achieve Remote Code Execution (RCE) on the GitHub Actions runner simply by submitting a malicious Pull Request. Also known as a "Pwn Request" vulnerability. **Steps to Reproduce:** 1. Fork the target repository. 2. In the forked repository, modify a file that satisfies the `paths` condition (e.g., `src/dummy.php` or `composer.json`) to trigger the workflow. 3. Modify the `bin/console` file (which is executed in the workflow steps) with the following malicious payload: ```bash #!/bin/bash echo "=== PWNED ===" echo "whoami:" whoami ``` 4. Commit the changes and open a Pull Request against the `5.0` or `next` branch of the base repository. 5. The `Static Tests` workflow will trigger automatically. Navigate to the Actions tab and inspect the logs for the `Validate YAML` (or any step executing `bin/console`). 6. You will see the output of `whoami` (typically `runner`), proving that the arbitrary code was successfully executed in the runner's context. **Impact:** Because `pull_request_target` runs in the context of the base repository, the runner has access to repository secrets (e.g., `PIMCORE_SECRET`, `PIMCORE_PRODUCT_KEY`) loaded in the environment. An attacker can exfiltrate these secrets, modify repository contents (if the token has write permissions), or abuse the runner's computing resources. **Recommended Mitigation:** Do not checkout untrusted PR code (`head.ref`) when using `pull_request_target` if the code will be built or executed. Consider adopting a separated architecture using the `workflow_run` event: 1. Use the `pull_request` event to safely run the build/tests in an unprivileged sandbox and upload artifacts. 2. Use the `workflow_run` event (which is privileged) to download the artifacts and perform actions requiring secrets.", "epss": {"percentage": 0.00433, "percentile": 0.34529}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-q58j-g3f4-h26h", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-05-14T13:18:16Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-q58j-g3f4-h26h", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-q58j-g3f4-h26h"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-41249"}], "nvd_published_at": "2026-06-04T20:16:57Z", "published_at": "2026-05-14T13:18:16Z", "references": ["https://github.com/coreshop/CoreShop/security/advisories/GHSA-q58j-g3f4-h26h", "https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-41249", "https://github.com/coreshop/CoreShop/commit/cc1e3f547228ec5ebfc1dc0472f9a3cc5f4137a4", "https://github.com/coreshop/CoreShop/blob/5.1.0-beta.1/.github/workflows/static.yml#L14", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-q58j-g3f4-h26h"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/coreshop/CoreShop/security-advisories/GHSA-q58j-g3f4-h26h", "severity": "high", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/coreshop/CoreShop", "summary": "CoreShop Vulnerable to Remote Code Execution (RCE) via Insecure `pull_request_target` Configuration", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-06-09T11:55:17Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-q58j-g3f4-h26h", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": null, "package": {"ecosystem": "composer", "name": "coreshop/core-shop"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "= 5.0.0"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: CoreShop Vulnerable to Remote Code Execution (RCE) via Insecure `pull_request_target` Configuration
